stem: A right circular cylinder has a base diameter of 14 centimeters and a height of 5 centimeters. What is the volume, in cubic centimeters, of the cylinder?
rationale: Choice A is correct. The volume V of a right circular cylinder is V = πr2h. Since the base diameter is 14 cm, the radius is r = 7 cm. With h = 5 cm, V = π(7)2(5) = 245π cubic centimeters. Choice B uses the diameter times height; Choice C uses 2 × radius × height; Choice D mistakenly uses the diameter as the radius.
